name: Christian McBride
description: American jazz bassist, composer, and arranger
abstract: Christian McBride (born May 31, 1972) is an American jazz bassist, bandleader, composer. He has appeared on more than 400 recordings as a sideman, and is an eleven-time Grammy Award winner.

interviews.title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: I had the great pleasure of recording with Christian McBride and Billy Drummond and we did a trio record called “Hidden Treasures.”

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: one of my most successful early records was “Dear Mr. Cole” with Benny Green and Christian McBride. And we never worked together.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Sands
about_quotes.evidence: Like being in Christian McBride’s band he really taught me his way of presenting music and what he wanted

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: We got 25 strings of the Montreal Symphony Orchestra, Billy Higgins, Christian McBride, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Wolff
about_quotes.evidence: I go to jazz festivals... Christian McBride. When you have a Christian McBride at a festival like this... I learned how much more to enjoy and appreciate him and his group after listening to him in person last night. It was the first time I had listened to him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Wolff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: And I remember we did a concert some years ago, Christian McBride. And there was this fuzzy-haired guy standing in the back of the concert hall... So afterwards he comes to the dressing room. He says, “Carl, man, I’m just really, really impressed.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013
